game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Ta Mère En Slip:** This is a party guessing game for 2 to 12 players, where one active player holds the iOS device to their forehead, displaying a randomly generated combination of a "character" and an "action" card (e.g., "a singer" + "lighting a fire") that only other players can see. Other players provide verbal clues to help the active player guess the combination without directly revealing the words, while the active player swipes down to pass on a difficult combination or swipes up to confirm a correct guess, advancing to the next card combination. Each round is governed by a customizable timer, which can be set for durations like 30, 60, or 90 seconds, and the objective is to correctly guess as many combinations as possible before time expires. The game features over 300 unique card combinations, with players able to add custom cards, and the winner is typically the player or team with the highest score after a predetermined number of rounds or total correct guesses.
game_info.description: Guess the Word! A single-player adaptation of the party game where you need to guess word combinations before time runs out. Each round presents a Character + Action combination. You'll see clues and must press the correct keys to indicate if you know it (UP ARROW) or want to skip (DOWN ARROW). Race against time to guess as many combinations as possible to achieve the highest score!
game_info.controls:
UP ARROW: Confirm correct guess (advances to next card)
DOWN ARROW: Pass/skip current combination
ENTER: Start game from start screen
ESC: Pause/unpause during gameplay
R: Return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and action testing. Validates that the game responds to UP and DOWN arrow inputs correctly, processes card combinations, and updates the score appropriately.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Alternates between UP (correct) and DOWN (skip) actions with random timing to test both success and skip mechanics. Ensures the game can handle various input patterns.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The game should process all inputs correctly, cycle through multiple cards, update score for correct guesses, and handle skips without errors. Test runs for a reasonable duration to validate stability.

TEST_2.test_description: Optimal win strategy testing. Tests the best possible gameplay to achieve maximum score by always answering correctly as quickly as possible.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Always selects UP arrow (correct guess) with minimal delay between cards to maximize the number of correct answers within the time limit. Simulates a perfect player.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Should achieve a high score by the end of the round, demonstrating the game's scoring system works correctly and that a skilled player can win. Should reach GAME_OVER_WIN state with maximum possible score.

TEST_3.test_description: Skip-heavy gameplay testing. Tests game behavior when player frequently skips difficult combinations.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Primarily uses DOWN arrow to skip most cards, occasionally using UP arrow to test mixed gameplay patterns and ensure skip functionality doesn't break scoring.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Game should handle many skips gracefully, maintain low but non-zero score, and complete the round normally. Tests that skipping doesn't cause errors or infinite loops.
